architecture_fit.note: Minimal delta: one attention QK dot-product fed to a sharp sigmoid, no MLP/extra layers, directly answers the sign-detector question.

baseline_comparison.note: Compares against the linear max(0,cos) ramp strawman in both metric (lift 9.55) and the sweep plot.

faithfulness.note: Purely hand-coded so no causal check on a real model; README claims a beta-slider ablation that app.py does not actually implement.

operating_range.note: Clean step across the full canonical cosine range and adjustable n_pairs, but no test across dimension/scale beyond the demo regime.

hardcoded_weights_bonus.note: Explicit hand-set circuit (beta=50, threshold=0) reproduces the sign detector by construction, though beta is chosen to directly equal the sharpness metric.

visual_judgement.note: Single-slice histogram explorer plus dual-axis canonical sweep makes the sharp flip at cos=0 vs linear ramp legible.

visualisation_rationale.note: Why-this-viz section ties chart choices to the three claims (flip exists, localised at 0, beats strawman) with right axes.

notes: A clean, minimal hand-coded sign detector (sigmoid of 50*dot) that hits a near-perfect threshold at 0 and beats the linear baseline ~9.5x, with a legible sweep visualisation. The main weaknesses are faithfulness: it is hand-set with no causal/ablation evidence and the README claims an interactive beta-slider ablation that app.py never implements, and the chosen beta=50 directly equals the reported sharpness metric. Solid architecture and baseline work make it borderline rather than a fail.
